title: Campaigns With Over 100 Conversions
schema: campaigns(campaign_id, campaign_name, start_date, end_date), conversions(conversion_id, campaign_id, conversion_date)
solution:
-- Conversions per campaign, keeping only the busy ones (>100 conversions).
SELECT
  c.campaign_name,
  COUNT(co.conversion_id) AS conversion_count
FROM conversions co
LEFT JOIN campaigns c ON co.campaign_id = c.campaign_id
GROUP BY c.campaign_name
HAVING COUNT(co.conversion_id) > 100;

title: Average Order-to-Delivery Time per Customer
solution:
-- SQLite version (uses julianday() because SQLite lacks DATEDIFF).
-- For SQL Server, swap with:
--   AVG(DATEDIFF(day, order_date, delivery_date))
WITH per_order AS (
  SELECT
    customer_id,
    julianday(delivery_date) - julianday(order_date) AS days_to_deliver
  FROM orders
)
SELECT
  customer_id,
  ROUND(AVG(days_to_deliver), 2) AS avg_delivery_days
FROM per_order
GROUP BY customer_id
ORDER BY avg_delivery_days DESC;

title: Pass Rate per Course and Semester
solution:
-- Conditional aggregation: count of passes / total students, per group.
SELECT
  course_id,
  semester,
  100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN grade >= 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(student_id)
    AS pass_rate_pct
FROM student_courses
GROUP BY course_id, semester
ORDER BY course_id, semester;

title: Properties With Over 50 Views in October 2024
schema: properties(property_id, property_name, location), property_views(view_id, property_id, view_date)
solution:
-- Views per property in October 2024, keeping only the popular ones (>50).
SELECT
  p.property_name,
  COUNT(pv.view_id) AS total_views
FROM property_views pv
LEFT JOIN properties p ON pv.property_id = p.property_id
WHERE pv.view_date BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-31'
GROUP BY p.property_name
HAVING COUNT(pv.view_id) > 50
ORDER BY total_views DESC;

title: Patients With More Than Two Missed Appointments
schema: patients(patient_id, patient_name), appointments(appointment_id, patient_id, appointment_date, status)
solution:
-- Count missed appointments per patient over the Aug–Oct 2024 window
-- and keep only the ones that missed more than two.
SELECT
  p.patient_name,
  COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS missed_appointments
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id
WHERE a.appointment_date BETWEEN '2024-08-01' AND '2024-10-31'
  AND a.status = 'Missed'
GROUP BY p.patient_name
HAVING COUNT(a.appointment_id) > 2
ORDER BY missed_appointments DESC;
